source_reviews.notes: The reopened EPA fact sheet supports the AAI environmental-professional qualifications and professional-judgment role. It supports the direction of credential-based moat and durable regulated work, but it does not measure 541620 pricing, pass-through, or the selected capture values.

The reopened EBJ page supports current AI-enabled productivity differentiation, policy sensitivity, resilient programmatic demand, and a fourth large consolidation wave led by strategics and PE platforms. Its broader C&E scope does not establish the selected adoption percentage, six-year t50, buyer count, pricing mix, or 541620 exit multiple.

The reopened 40-page ACEC report supports document search, proposal writing, knowledge retrieval, specification drafting, legacy-data review, uneven maturity, and an incremental five-to-ten-year transformation constrained by regulation, contracts, culture, procurement, and liability. It is AEC evidence rather than a 541620 task-automation or effective-adoption measurement.

The reopened Equiteq page supports 615 ESG-consulting deals since 2021, approximately 56% PE or PE-backed participation, 73% of acquired firms below 100 employees, fragmentation, and an approximately 11.3x private-market median. Its global ESG population is not a direct small-US-541620 comp set and does not establish the selected 5.5x entry, 8.0x exit, or six active consolidators.

The reopened product page confirms a 2025 US AEC owner and principal study covering ownership and demographics, including environmental consulting in its stated industry scope. The public page exposes neither a 541620 owner-age statistic nor evidence of a succession shortage, so the 30% selection remains a disclosed planning estimate and the shortage boolean is conservatively false.

The reopened BLS page supports environmental data collection and analysis, hazard assessment, reporting, regulatory guidance, fieldwork, continued consulting demand, and 4% projected employment growth from 2024 to 2034. It is occupational evidence rather than a 541620 industry survey and does not measure the selected automatable fractions.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 4-digit 5416 role mix is a severe proxy mismatch for 541620 and omits the actual environmental-science, geology, engineering, and field-technician core.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: S3 covers AEC firms and S6 is occupational evidence; neither measures task automation in lower-middle-market US 541620 firms.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: These are LLM/workflow estimates and do not count physical field automation as immediate labor replacement.
inputs.pi_dist: No reopened source provides a NAICS 541620 billing-mix or AI-savings pass-through measurement.
inputs.pi_dist: The selected value is a judgment across heterogeneous Phase I, permitting, remediation, monitoring, and advisory work.
inputs.pi_moat: This measures retention of savings, not the persistence of paid demand; the latter is assessed only in terminal demand.
inputs.pi_moat: Moat differs sharply by client program, geography, credentials, and subsegment.
inputs.t50_years: No representative survey measures 50% effective AI adoption for US NAICS 541620.
inputs.t50_years: The selected date excludes mere tool availability and requires material use in the labor-saving workflows scored in V.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: S2 and S3 do not publish a 541620 effective-adoption percentage; this is not a direct observation.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is an AEC analogy, not a quantitative historical adoption series for 541620.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No reopened source provides an extracted owner-age statistic for NAICS 541620 or the $1-10M EBITDA target band.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: False means not documented by the reopened evidence, not that transition risk is absent.
inputs.active_consolidators: The count is not a verified census and the cited markets include broader environmental engineering and global ESG consulting.
inputs.buy_mult: S4 is global and broader than US environmental consulting and its reported median is not a small-target entry comp.
inputs.buy_mult: Small-firm figures require EBITDA normalization after market-rate owner compensation.
inputs.exit_mult: No reopened source reports directly comparable US 541620 entry and exit transactions.
inputs.exit_mult: The selected spread is a roll-up hypothesis, not evidence that multiple expansion will occur.
inputs.pricing_structure: No reopened public source reports a NAICS 541620 revenue split across T&M, fixed-fee, and cost-reimbursable contracts.
summary: Mechanics pass exactly and all six score-driving sources were accessible when reopened. The sources support the useful directional thesis that environmental consulting has durable regulated and field-dependent work, meaningful document and data automation opportunities, partial capture, and active consolidation, but the score relies on openly disclosed 5416, AEC, ESG, and occupational proxies plus several unmeasured estimates. Those limitations warrant publication caveats but do not constitute a material research defect or reverse the thesis.
